name: The First System
description: Pell has found black crystals in the vault that predate the Grid and suggest a prior technology. The Elder People archive at the monastery library may hold the pre-Grid documentation she needs to understand what they stored.
accept_text: She writes the reference on a torn piece of paper from her notebook with the care of someone who has been composing this request in her head for three years. 'The Elder archive at the Monastery.' She folds the paper and hands it to you. 'I need their pre-Grid technical documentation — specifically anything that references what the Archive language calls the First Pattern or the Sealed Archive. Both are literal translations. The Elder archivist records use archaic script that I've been working through fragments of, and those are the terms that appear repeatedly in the documents I believe describe the technology this crystal represents.' She pauses. 'What I believe those documents describe: the Grid was built because the first system failed at something. I think the first system stored isolated consciousness — individual, preserved — and the Grid was built to connect what the storage system kept separate.' She looks at the black crystal. 'If I'm right, the black crystals in this vault are consciousness-storage media. Three hundred years old, older — potentially much older than the Grid.' She meets your eyes. 'The archivist at the monastery is Lyss. She knows the collection. She may know where the pre-Grid documentation is, and she may be more willing to discuss it than the monks will.' She pauses. 'Tell her what the crystals look like. She'll understand why it matters.'
complete_text: Lyss reads the description on the paper with the focused stillness of someone cross-referencing against a very large internal catalogue. Then she looks up. 'The First Pattern,' she says. 'I know what she's looking for. Brother Kell found it three months ago — that's what changed in his research, that's what he found in the records that he hasn't told me yet.' She goes to the shelves. 'The pre-Grid technical documentation is in the Occasion of the Sealed Chamber — which is not an occasion I had a reference for until six months ago, when Kell's work with the cipher gave us access to the oldest section of the collection.' She pulls a wrapped bundle from the shelf and sets it on the reading table. 'Three documents. Partial. The most complete one is approximately seven hundred years old.' She unwraps it carefully. 'I can translate the relevant sections for you. But I need to know first: Pell said the black crystals responded to the Grid reactivation eight months ago.' She looks at you. 'If the crystals responded — if what's stored in them responded — then there are things in those crystals that have been waiting in isolation for the network to come back.' She looks at the document. 'That changes what the pre-Grid documentation means. It stopped being history eight months ago.' She begins to translate, slowly, reading aloud: the first system, the sealed archive, the decision to build a network instead of a vault — and what the architects of the Grid intended for what they left behind in the vault when they moved on.'
